text.title: Handoff Spec Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Handoff Spec Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every interactive component lists all 8 states (default, hover, active, focus, disabled, loading, error, empty); absent states are rejected unless explicitly marked N/A with a one-line reason.
rule.rationale: Missing states are the #1 source of handoff loss; explicit N/A keeps the matrix complete.

rule.statement: All colors, spacing, typography, and motion values reference design tokens by name (e.g. color.primary.500, spacing.md); raw hex / px values are rejected.
rule.rationale: Raw values break dark-mode + theming and create drift between spec and code.

rule.statement: Every motion spec names duration in ms and an easing curve (CSS keyword or cubic-bezier). 'Smooth' / 'fast' alone are rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague motion descriptions force engineers to guess; concrete values produce deterministic feel.

rule.statement: Every spec carries focus-order annotation + computed contrast ratios on every text/background pair; contrast ratios below WCAG AA threshold (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large) are flagged for designer review.
rule.rationale: Missing a11y annotations push the work to eng or get skipped entirely; computed ratios force the designer to fix in design, not patch in code.

rule.statement: Every spec names at least 3 edge cases (long text overflow, RTL, slow-network, no-data, error) with the visual treatment for each; specs without edge cases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Edge cases drive most production bugs; naming them in the spec moves the conversation upstream.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-handoff-spec-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
